rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR. VS. HEALTHRIGHT 360 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANT VITKA EISEN TO FURTHER RESPOND TO SROGS3 AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTION; DECLARATION; AND, MEMORANDUM.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 2, 2026, Line 8, PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANT VITKA EISEN TO FURTHER RESPOND TO SROGS3 AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTION. Hearing required. The court's indicated order appears below. This case has been marked by numerous discovery disputes. Plaintiff's requests appear repetitive and burdensome but the court cannot ascertain from Defendant's papers in opposition to this motion whether Eisen has adequately responded to prior requests that she claims these requests duplicate. The court continues the motion for hearing to May 5, 2026 and orders Eisen to submit a supplemental declaration that attaches her responses to all of Plaintiff's discovery. Where a response has been amended, Eisen need not attach the original response. The court further orders Plaintiff to cc Eisen's counsel's assistant on all emailed correspondence, as counsel has previously requested, to facilitate timely resolution of discovery disputes. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).
